questions.question: Should we create a shared UseQueryResult mock helper in helpers.tsx to fix Portfolio.test.tsx, or also consolidate the duplicate defaultQueryState helpers in MarketDetail.test.tsx and DiscussionThread.test.tsx (which have the same missing-props issue)?
questions.why_it_matters: The exploration found 3 test files with inconsistent UseQueryResult mock approaches all missing the same 5 properties. Fixing only Portfolio.test.tsx leaves the other two broken. A shared helper in helpers.tsx would fix all three and prevent future drift, but the task description only mentions Portfolio.test.tsx.

questions.question: For chartUtils.test.ts line 12 (process.env.TZ = 'UTC'), should we add @types/node to devDependencies or switch to import.meta.env (Vite's approach)?
questions.why_it_matters: Adding @types/node is simpler but pulls in all Node.js type definitions which may conflict with the browser-targeted frontend. Using import.meta.env is more Vite-idiomatic but import.meta.env doesn't support TZ (it's not a real env var replacement). A third option is a targeted type declaration (declare var process: ...) in the test setup.

questions.question: The tsconfig.json has noUncheckedIndexedAccess: true, which is the root cause of both the ProbabilityBar.test.tsx (Element|undefined) and api-client.test.ts (Object possibly undefined) errors. Should we fix these with non-null assertions (!) or proper type guards?
questions.why_it_matters: Non-null assertions (bars[0]!) are concise but suppress the safety check. Type guards (if (bars[0]) { ... }) are safer but verbose in tests where we know the elements exist. The task description suggests either approach; the right choice affects code quality.

questions.question: Are MarketDetail.test.tsx and DiscussionThread.test.tsx also failing the build, or are they excluded from type-checking?
questions.why_it_matters: The exploration found these files have the same missing UseQueryResult properties as Portfolio.test.tsx. If they also fail the build, fixing only the 4 files in the task description won't achieve 'pnpm build passes'. Need to verify the actual full error list.

assumptions.assumption: The 5 missing UseQueryResult properties (isFetched, isFetchedAfterMount, isInitialLoading, isPaused, isEnabled) are the complete set needed for React Query v5.60+
assumptions.if_wrong: We'd still get type errors after adding these 5. Need to check the actual UseQueryResult type definition from the installed @tanstack/react-query version.

assumptions.assumption: Non-null assertions (!) are acceptable in test files for array index access since we control the test DOM and mock data
assumptions.if_wrong: Would need to use type guards or helper functions that narrow the type, adding more verbosity to tests.

assumptions.assumption: Adding @types/node to devDependencies won't break the frontend build or introduce type conflicts with DOM types
assumptions.if_wrong: Could cause type conflicts between Node.js and DOM globals. Would need a targeted type declaration instead.

assumptions.assumption: pnpm build runs tsc --noEmit (or equivalent) which type-checks test files
assumptions.if_wrong: If build only checks src/ (not __tests__/), these errors wouldn't block the build. But CI likely runs a separate type-check that includes tests.

assumptions.assumption: The 29+ errors from the exploration are the complete set; no additional errors will surface after fixing these
assumptions.if_wrong: Fixing these may uncover previously-shadowed errors. Will need to iterate until pnpm build passes cleanly.
